title: Public acceptability of carbon pricing: Unravelling the impact of revenue recycling

tax_mentions.context: Systematic review of public attitudes toward carbon taxation and how alternative revenue recycling options shape acceptability, support and willingness to pay.
Defines fuel and gasoline taxes as implicit carbon pricing and includes studies on public support for such tax increases and revenue use.
tax_mentions.notes: Fuel/gas taxes treated as carbon pricing instruments.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Quantity-based emissions trading scheme (cap-and-trade), not a tax on fuels or carbon.
Umbrella term covering both carbon taxes and ETS; not itself a specific tax instrument.

inclusion_reason: Systematic PRISMA review synthesizing determinants of public acceptability, support and WTP for carbon taxation, fuel and gasoline taxes, especially design of revenue recycling, across 48 empirical studies.
